summary: Static validation of the alpha.15 TitleBar.jsx rewrite (iter-26). Customer's iter-25 alpha.14 fix did NOT resolve the button-no-op bug on real Windows, so TitleBar.jsx was rewritten from scratch with explicit console logging, defensive onMouseDown stopPropagation, lazy dynamic import of @tauri-apps/api/window, and direct IPC calls with try/catch. Verified all 16 file-level invariants from the review request via a new pytest suite (18 test items including parametrised data-testid checks), plus iter-25 regression guards (capability grant, sidecar kill, CSS pointer-events rule), version stamping, and a live backend smoke. All 18 tests PASS. ESLint on TitleBar.jsx is clean. Per the review instructions, no Playwright was attempted: the chromeless title bar is gated on window.__TAURI__ which is undefined in the dev sandbox — real verification only happens on a Windows alpha.15 install.
action_items: Ship alpha.15 installer — alpha.14 binaries on the customer's machine STILL contain the bug. Surface a clear customer instruction: download alpha.15 (not refresh), then open DevTools (Ctrl+Shift+I or right-click → Inspect → Console) BEFORE clicking the X/min/max so the new console.log/console.error lines are captured if the bug persists.

After alpha.15 ships, ask the customer to paste the full DevTools console output. Expected on a click: `[TitleBar] minimize clicked` then either `[TitleBar] minimize OK` (fixed), `[TitleBar] no window handle available for minimize` (window-API resolution failure → look at withGlobalTauri / capability), or `[TitleBar] minimize FAILED <stringified error>` (IPC rejected → permission or backend issue). That output will pinpoint which of the 4 remaining failure modes to chase next.

On the alpha.15 build, also manually verify: (a) X reaps the sidecar (tasklist /FI "IMAGENAME eq bighat-backend.exe" empty after close), (b) brand area still drags the window, (c) maximize button toggles to restore icon state (isMax listener works).
critical_code_review_comments: TitleBar.jsx is well-structured: helpers (inTauri, getWin, callWin) are isolated and Btn is a thin wrapper — easy to reason about. The dynamic-import fallback chain (mod.getCurrentWindow || mod.getCurrent → window.__TAURI__.window.getCurrentWindow) covers both Tauri v2 conventions and the global-injection path; good belt-and-suspenders.

The `console.log('[TitleBar] <name> clicked')` BEFORE any await ensures we'll see the click registered even if the dynamic import itself hangs — that's the right diagnostic ordering for the customer-reported symptom (button does nothing). Keep it.

Defensive onMouseDown stopPropagation is correct in spirit but worth noting: it only protects against a drag-region attribute creeping in on an ancestor. The outer .tauri-titlebar wrapper already correctly OMITS data-tauri-drag-region (iter-25 fix preserved), so this is purely belt-and-suspenders. Leave it in — it costs nothing.

Potential blind spot if alpha.15 still fails on real Windows: the OS-level `-webkit-app-region: drag` on .tauri-titlebar (index.css line ~1014) applies to the WHOLE bar including children. Buttons opt out via `-webkit-app-region: no-drag` (line ~1090) but this CSS-driven drag is handled by the OS chrome layer, NOT by Tauri's JS drag-region handler. If the customer's WebView2 build is mis-handling no-drag, NO amount of JS event manipulation will save us. Worth asking the customer for their WebView2 version (`reg query "HKLM\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\Microsoft\EdgeUpdate\Clients\{F3017226-FE2A-4295-8BDF-00C3A9A7E4C5}" /v pv`) if alpha.15 still no-ops.
Minor: the `chrono_now()` epoch:<secs> timestamp format in lib.rs is unchanged from iter-25 — still harder to scan when correlating with the customer's DevTools console timestamps. Optional polish.

Minor: TitleBar.jsx has no error boundary around the useEffect's async IIFE — if `w.onResized` itself synchronously throws (rare but possible in older webview builds) the unmount cleanup would set `unlisten = null` and silently swallow it. Current try/catch covers the await chain so this is theoretical, not actionable.

success_rate.backend: 100% (18/18 — 16 static contract + iter-25 regression + version + live smoke)
success_rate.frontend: n/a — Tauri-only fix, sandbox cannot render window.__TAURI__-gated component

context_for_next_testing_agent: The alpha.15 fix is purely a TitleBar.jsx rewrite — the iter-25 fixes (index.css pointer-events + capability grant + lib.rs kill path) are UNCHANGED and verified still in place by TestIter25RegressionInvariants. If a future iter touches TitleBar.jsx, the pytest gate covers: dynamic import of @tauri-apps/api/window, all 3 IPC methods present, all 3 data-testids, onMouseDown stopPropagation, console.log + console.error logging, inTauri() early-return-null, NO data-tauri-drag-region on outer wrapper, drag-region preserved on .tauri-titlebar__brand. Real verification still requires a Windows installer build (sandbox cannot exercise window.__TAURI__). The new console.log/console.error statements are deliberately customer-facing — they ARE the diagnostic surface for alpha.15.
rca of the issue: iter-25 RCA was correct in mechanism (SVG event-target → drag handler eating click) but possibly incomplete in scope. Hypotheses still on the table for why alpha.14 didn't fix it on customer's Windows: (1) WebView2 quirk where `-webkit-app-region: no-drag` on .tauri-titlebar__btn isn't being honoured — the OS-level chrome drag is intercepting mousedown BEFORE the JS event reaches the React handler. (2) The @tauri-apps/api/window static import was resolving against a stale chunk so getCurrent()/minimize() were undefined at runtime; alpha.15 switches to dynamic-import + global fallback to dodge this. (3) A permission-side reject from the v2 capability layer despite `core:window:default` being granted — the new try/catch + console.error around .minimize()/.toggleMaximize()/.close() will surface this in DevTools. Mitigation in this iter is diagnostic-first: ship alpha.15 with explicit logging so the next customer report contains the actual failure path, not just 'buttons do nothing'.
